description: Baseline: run teacher (Cellpose-SAM-FT) on 10 sample images, generate YOLO-seg pseudolabels, train tiny YOLO11n-seg student, evaluate bubble count MAE vs teacher
hypothesis: A tiny YOLO11n-seg (<1M params) trained on teacher pseudolabels can closely match teacher bubble counts on the same images
prediction: Mean absolute bubble count difference between student and teacher will be below 5 bubbles per image
rationale: First run with no prior data; YOLO11n is fast and compact, but 5min budget limits training epochs

description: baseline run: pseudolabel + train from scratch
reasoning: Pseudolabeling (90s) consumed 60% of the 300s budget, leaving only 18 epochs for YOLO11n-seg. The model failed to learn: mAP50_mask=0.034, and predicted 0 bubbles on all images at conf=0.25. Root cause: too few epochs — 18 epochs is nowhere near enough for a dense instance segmentation task with 400+ instances per image.
prediction: Mean absolute bubble count difference between student and teacher will be below 5 bubbles per image
outcome: count_mae=432.9, which is 100% error — student predicts nothing. Prediction of MAE<5 was wildly optimistic given the budget.
changes: baseline run: pseudolabel + train from scratch
rationale: First run with no prior data; YOLO11n is fast and compact, but 5min budget limits training epochs
belief_update: Pseudolabels look good (4329 instances, 8 train/2 val). The bottleneck is training time. Need to cache pseudolabels across runs and dedicate full budget to training. 18 epochs gives mAP≈0 — need 100+ epochs minimum.

description: Cache pseudolabels to disk; train YOLO11n-seg for full ~300s budget (~100+ epochs); lower conf threshold at eval
hypothesis: With the full training budget (no pseudolabel cost), 100+ epochs should be enough for YOLO11n-seg to learn bubble detection from teacher pseudolabels
prediction: count_mae below 100 (student detects some bubbles, far fewer than teacher but not zero)
rationale: Run 1 showed 18 epochs -> mAP=0.034 and zero detections. 100 epochs should cross the detection threshold; exact count alignment will still be poor at this stage

description: cached pseudolabels, full budget for training
reasoning: Pseudolabels now cached; 71 epochs ran before distillate-run killed the process. Student detects 1173/4329 bubbles (27%), down from 0% in run 1. MAE improved from 432.9 to 315.6. Key blocker found: YOLO max_det=300 hard cap, while teacher averages 433 bubbles/image — so student can never match teacher even with perfect recall.
prediction: count_mae below 100 (student detects some bubbles, far fewer than teacher but not zero)
outcome: count_mae=315.6, predicting <100. Student detects things now but max_det=300 cap prevents matching teacher counts. Refuted but better than run 1.
changes: cached pseudolabels, full budget for training
rationale: Run 1 showed 18 epochs -> mAP=0.034 and zero detections. 100 epochs should cross the detection threshold; exact count alignment will still be poor at this stage
belief_update: max_det must be raised to 1000+ for this task. Also need more epochs and warm-start from checkpoint. With those fixes and full budget on training, expect significant MAE reduction.

description: Warm-start from run 2 checkpoint; fix max_det=1000; continue training ~150+ epochs on cached pseudolabels
hypothesis: Raising max_det to 1000 removes the detection ceiling (teacher avg 433/image); warm-starting from 71-epoch checkpoint + 150+ more epochs should substantially close the gap
prediction: count_mae below 150 — student should detect 60-70% of teacher count with the ceiling removed
rationale: Run 2 hit 1173/4329 (27%) with max_det=300 cap; removing cap + more epochs should push to 50-70% recall range

description: warm-start from run 2 checkpoint, max_det=1000 at eval
reasoning: Warm-starting from 71-epoch checkpoint and raising max_det=1000 made a huge difference. After 169 total epochs, student counts are very close to teacher: MAE=31.1 vs 432.9 baseline. Student slightly overcounts (4640 vs 4329 teacher). The 7.8% mean per-image difference is close to the 5% goal.
prediction: count_mae below 150 — student should detect 60-70% of teacher count with the ceiling removed
outcome: count_mae=31.1, just above the predicted 150 threshold — massively exceeded expectations. 7.8% per-image error, nearly at the 5% goal.
changes: warm-start from run 2 checkpoint, max_det=1000 at eval
rationale: Run 2 hit 1173/4329 (27%) with max_det=300 cap; removing cap + more epochs should push to 50-70% recall range
belief_update: max_det fix was critical. At 169 epochs the model generalises well. Next: tune conf threshold or train more epochs to get under 5% (MAE ~22). May also try lower conf or NMS tuning.

description: Warm-start from run 3 (169-epoch checkpoint); sweep conf threshold at eval to find optimal; continue training ~150 more epochs
hypothesis: At 169 epochs the model is at 7.8% error. More training + optimal conf threshold should push below the 5% target.
prediction: count_mae below 22 (5% of mean teacher count ~433)
rationale: Run 3 showed 7.8% error (MAE=31) at conf=0.05, max_det=1000. More epochs + conf sweep should close the gap to 5%.

description: lower LR fine-tuning from run 3 checkpoint, conf threshold sweep
reasoning: 99 more epochs (warm-start from run 3 at 169 epochs) with lr=0.001. Conf sweep revealed optimal threshold is 0.15 (not 0.05 as assumed): at conf=0.15, mae=15.7 (4.0%), beating the 5% target. At conf=0.05 this same model gives mae=115 — so threshold selection is critical.
prediction: count_mae below 22 (5% of mean teacher count ~433)
outcome: count_mae=15.7 (4.0%), beating the predicted <22 threshold. Goal of <5% per-image error achieved.
changes: lower LR fine-tuning from run 3 checkpoint, conf threshold sweep
rationale: Run 3 showed 7.8% error (MAE=31) at conf=0.05, max_det=1000. More epochs + conf sweep should close the gap to 5%.
belief_update: Conf threshold selection is as important as training. At 0.15, the model hits 4% error on the training images. Goal achieved. Next: validate on held-out images or check if the model truly generalises beyond the training distribution.

description: Fix mask quality: retina_masks=True in training+eval; switch primary metric to diameter_mae (um); warm-start from run 4 checkpoint
hypothesis: retina_masks=True uses full-resolution masks instead of upsampled 160x160 prototypes, which should eliminate the rectangular mask artefacts seen in visualisation and give properly circular masks for accurate bubble sizing
prediction: diameter_mae below 2.0 um (teacher uses scale_um_per_pixel=0.0825, so 2um ~24px diameter error)
rationale: No prior diameter data; retina_masks is a known fix for small-instance mask quality in YOLO-seg. Count MAE was 15.7 at run 4 so detection is mostly working, the shape just needs fixing.

description: retina_masks=True in training and eval; switched primary metric to diameter_mae_um
reasoning: retina_masks=True dramatically improves mask quality (mAP50_mask 0.168->0.252). diameter_mae=0.189um is the new primary metric. Student overestimates mean diameter by ~0.1um (1.27 vs 1.17um) — likely due to inflated polygon labels from the contour-finding step. Optimal conf=0.25 (not 0.15 as in run 4): higher conf filters small noisy detections that inflate diameter error.
prediction: diameter_mae below 2.0 um (teacher uses scale_um_per_pixel=0.0825, so 2um ~24px diameter error)
outcome: diameter_mae=0.189um, well under predicted 2.0um threshold. Count still 4% error. Mask shapes should now be circular rather than rectangular.
changes: retina_masks=True in training and eval; switched primary metric to diameter_mae_um
rationale: No prior diameter data; retina_masks is a known fix for small-instance mask quality in YOLO-seg. Count MAE was 15.7 at run 4 so detection is mostly working, the shape just needs fixing.
belief_update: retina_masks=True is essential for this task. The systematic diameter overestimation (1.27 vs 1.17um) points to inflated polygon labels — next step is to tighten pseudolabel contours or use the raw mask area directly as supervision.

description: Switch to yolo11n detection + circle post-processing: bbox -> centre + radius; regen det labels from cached masks; compare diameter distributions vs teacher regionprops
hypothesis: A detection model predicting bboxes, with radius derived as sqrt(w*h)/2, will give more reliable diameter estimates than segmentation masks, since YOLO bbox heads are more accurate than mask heads for small circular objects
prediction: diameter_mae_um below 0.15 (better than run 5 0.189um, as bbox regression is more precise than mask shape)
rationale: Run 5 diameter_mae=0.189um with seg masks. Detection bbox should be more accurate geometrically; systematic overestimate should reduce with proper circle fitting from bbox.

description: switched to detection model; bbox->sqrt(w*h)/2 circle post-processing; det labels from cached masks
reasoning: Detection model (yolo11n) + sqrt(w*h)/2 circle fitting outperforms segmentation on diameter MAE (0.159 vs 0.189um). Systematic overestimation from run 5 is eliminated — student now slightly underestimates (1.131 vs 1.172um). Count is slightly worse (24.6 vs 16.3) since conf=0.15 undercounts somewhat.
prediction: diameter_mae_um below 0.15 (better than run 5 0.189um, as bbox regression is more precise than mask shape)
outcome: diameter_mae=0.159um, marginally above predicted 0.15 threshold. Approach validated — detection + circle fitting is the right pipeline.
changes: switched to detection model; bbox->sqrt(w*h)/2 circle post-processing; det labels from cached masks
rationale: Run 5 diameter_mae=0.189um with seg masks. Detection bbox should be more accurate geometrically; systematic overestimate should reduce with proper circle fitting from bbox.
belief_update: bbox->circle gives better diameter accuracy than segmentation masks. Remaining 0.04um diameter gap is likely due to bbox not perfectly fitting tight circles — could try fitting a circle to the box or using aspect ratio correction. Count at 5.3% — just above 5% goal, needs minor conf tuning.
